mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, positive month-over-month growth in industrial production confirms resilient supply-side activity that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, producing negative 5-day forward returns in > 50% of positive INDPRO growth months, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation crosses above 215.0 while the federal funds rate remains elevated, the combination signals persistent stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL > 215.0 and FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, which mechanically supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with PAYEMS growth (Rank IC < 0.02), or the signal consistently underperforms a buy-and-hold baseline across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.0% quarter-over-quarter, it signals a contraction in aggregate demand that precedes earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to predict negative returns, with SPX producing positive 20-day returns in > 70% of sub-1.0% GDP growth quarters, or the signal exhibits high false-positive rates in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate a synchronized supply-labor recovery that boosts short-term market sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to generate positive 5-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.
